mission.title: General Kubernetes recovery workflow for UNKNOWN_EXECUTION_FAILURE
mission.description: Catch-all diagnostic mission for when the console preflight reports UNKNOWN_EXECUTION_FAILURE. It gathers context, checks cluster access, inspects events and failing pods, reviews logs, and provides a clean recovery path instead of blind retries.

steps.title: Capture the failing context
steps.description: Record the current kubectl context and any command that failed so you can correlate later findings with the exact environment and namespace.
commands:
echo "LAST_COMMAND=${LAST_COMMAND:-not-set}"
kubectl config current-context
kubectl config view --minify

steps.title: Check API server reachability first
steps.description: Confirm the failure is not caused by a dead control plane, expired credentials, or a missing kubeconfig before chasing workload-specific symptoms.

steps.title: Inspect recent warning events
steps.description: Read the newest warning events across the cluster or in TARGET_NAMESPACE to surface scheduling, image pull, RBAC, and admission failures quickly.
commands:
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}"
if test -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"; then
  kubectl get events -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20
else
  kubectl get events -A --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20
fi

steps.title: Inspect failing pods and workloads
steps.description: List non-running pods so you can see the exact state transition and reason fields.
commands:
TARGET_NAMESPACE="${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}"
if test -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"; then
  kubectl get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE"
  kubectl get pods -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" --field-selector=status.phase!=Running
else
  kubectl get pods -A
  kubectl get pods -A --field-selector=status.phase!=Running
fi

steps.title: Read logs from the affected workload
steps.description: If TARGET_NAMESPACE and TARGET_POD are known, fetch recent logs to capture application-level errors. This is usually the fastest way to turn an unknown execution failure into a specific root cause.
commands:
if test -n "${TARGET_NAMESPACE:-}" && test -n "${TARGET_POD:-}"; then
  kubectl logs -n "$TARGET_NAMESPACE" "$TARGET_POD" --tail=100
else
  echo "Set TARGET_NAMESPACE and TARGET_POD to fetch workload logs"
fi

steps.title: Retry only after the root cause is identified
steps.description: Once you have a specific cause from events or logs, rerun the failed operation and verify that the symptom is gone. Avoid repeated blind retries because they hide transient and authorization problems.
commands:
if test -n "${LAST_COMMAND:-}"; then
  echo "Re-run after fixing the root cause: $LAST_COMMAND"
else
  echo "Re-run the original operation only after applying the targeted fix"
fi

troubleshooting.title: Failure turns out to be an RBAC problem
troubleshooting.description: Switch to the rbac-audit runbook and validate the exact forbidden verb and resource with kubectl auth can-i before making access changes.

troubleshooting.title: Failure is caused by missing kubeconfig credentials
troubleshooting.description: Switch to the credentials-setup mission to restore ~/.kube/config or the exec plugin credentials before retrying the original operation.

resolution.summary: This mission turns UNKNOWN_EXECUTION_FAILURE into a concrete next step by gathering cluster state, warning events, failing workloads, and logs before retrying the original action.

prerequisites.resources: Cluster read access; pod log access is recommended.
prerequisites.description: Set TARGET_NAMESPACE, TARGET_POD, and LAST_COMMAND when you already know which workload or action failed.
